authorRole: Product Manager at a tech startup
authorRoleZh: 科技创业公司产品经理
title: How Faith Changed the Way I Lead
titleZh: 信仰如何改变了我的领导方式
excerpt: I used to think leadership meant being the smartest person in the room. My faith taught me it means being the most caring.
excerptZh: 我曾经以为领导力意味着做房间里最聪明的人。信仰教会我，领导力意味着做最关怀他人的人。
content:
I discovered faith during my MBA at an American university. A neighbor invited me to a small group — I went for the free food, honestly. But what I found was a community that genuinely cared about each other.

When I returned to Shanghai and joined a startup, I was thrown into a leadership role. The default mode in Chinese tech is: move fast, push hard, results above everything. And I was good at that.

But something had shifted in me. I started leading differently — not softer, but deeper. I invested in my team members as people, not just resources. I was honest about my own limitations instead of pretending to have all the answers.

My colleagues noticed. 'Why are you different?' they'd ask. And that opened doors for real conversations — about purpose, about meaning, about what we're actually living for.

The hardest part was when my boss asked me to inflate user metrics for an investor meeting. My old self would have done it without thinking. Instead, I pushed back — gently, with alternative ways to present our genuine growth. It cost me political capital. But my team saw it. And their trust in me deepened.

Three years in, I've built a team culture that's remarkably different from the industry standard. People stay longer. They're more creative. They support each other. I can't claim credit for all of it, but I know that the values I learned in that small group in America are at the root.

If you're a returnee wondering whether your faith matters in the workplace — it does. Not as a megaphone, but as a foundation.
contentZh:
我在美国读MBA期间发现了信仰。一个邻居邀请我参加小组——说实话，我是冲着免费食物去的。但我发现了一个真正关心彼此的社区。

回到上海加入一家创业公司后，我被推到了领导岗位。中国科技圈的默认模式是：快速行动，大力推进，结果高于一切。我很擅长这个。

但我内心发生了变化。我开始用不同的方式领导——不是更软弱，而是更深入。我把团队成员当作人来投资，而不仅仅是资源。我诚实地面对自己的局限，而不是假装什么都懂。

同事们注意到了。「你为什么不一样？」他们会问。这为真实的对话打开了大门——关于目的、关于意义、关于我们究竟为什么而活。

最难的时候是老板让我为投资人会议夸大用户数据。以前的我会不假思索地去做。但我选择了委婉地推回——提供了展示我们真实增长的替代方案。这让我付出了政治资本的代价。但我的团队看到了。他们对我的信任加深了。

三年过去了，我建立了一种与行业标准截然不同的团队文化。人们待得更久。他们更有创造力。他们互相支持。我不能把功劳都归于自己，但我知道，我在美国那个小组里学到的价值观是根基。

如果你是一个海归，想知道信仰在职场是否重要——它重要。不是作为扩音器，而是作为根基。

title: Finding My Identity Beyond My Resume
titleZh: 在简历之外找到我的身份
excerpt: In China's achievement-driven culture, my worth was my output. Faith helped me see myself differently.
excerptZh: 在中国以成就为导向的文化中，我的价值等于我的产出。信仰帮助我以不同的方式看待自己。
content:
Growing up in China, I was taught that my value comes from what I achieve. Good grades. Good university. Good job. Good salary. The ladder goes on forever.

When I went to America for my CS degree, I met people who lived differently. They worked hard, yes — but their identity wasn't chained to their performance. I was fascinated and skeptical.

Through a series of conversations, a Bible study, and the patience of a mentor named David, I began to understand a different way of seeing myself. My value wasn't earned — it was given.

Coming back to Shenzhen and joining Huawei was a test. The 996 culture. The constant competition. The pressure to define myself by my title and salary. Old habits die hard.

But something had changed at my core. When I got passed over for a promotion, I was disappointed — but I wasn't destroyed. When a colleague took credit for my work, I was frustrated — but I didn't retaliate. When I saw a junior engineer struggling, I helped instead of competing.

My colleagues think I'm unusually calm. My manager says I'm the most collaborative person on the team. They don't know the full story yet. But some have started asking questions.

The journey isn't easy. Some days the old identity pulls hard — the need to prove, to achieve, to be seen. But I keep coming back to what I learned in that small apartment Bible study in America: I am loved not for what I do, but for who I am.

To any returnee reading this in the middle of a 996 week: you are more than your output. You are more than your title. And there are others in your city who understand this too.
contentZh:
在中国长大，我被教导价值来自于成就。好成绩。好大学。好工作。好薪水。这个阶梯永远没有尽头。

去美国读CS学位时，我遇到了活法不同的人。他们也努力工作——但他们的身份不被表现所束缚。我既着迷又怀疑。

通过一系列对话、查经班和一位叫David的导师的耐心，我开始理解一种不同的看待自己的方式。我的价值不是挣来的——而是被给予的。

回到深圳加入华为是一个考验。996文化。持续的竞争。被头衔和薪水定义自己的压力。旧习惯很难改变。

但我内心最深处已经改变了。当我错过一次晋升时，我失望——但没有被击垮。当同事抢了我的功劳时，我沮丧——但没有报复。当我看到一个初级工程师在苦苦挣扎时，我选择帮助而不是竞争。

同事们觉得我出奇地平静。经理说我是团队里最具协作精神的人。他们还不知道全部的故事。但有些人已经开始问问题了。

这段旅程不容易。有些日子旧的身份认同拉力很强——需要证明、需要成就、需要被看到。但我一次又一次回到我在美国那个小公寓查经班里学到的：我被爱不是因为我做了什么，而是因为我是谁。

对任何在996中读到这篇文章的海归：你不仅仅是你的产出。你不仅仅是你的头衔。在你的城市里，也有其他人理解这一点。
